# How to connect OpenClaw to Discord: Integration guide. How to connect OpenClaw to Discord: Integration guide - How to connect OpenClaw to Discord: Integration guide. You can keep it in one channel for quick Q&A or let it help with ops, releases or support. If you connect OpenClaw and let it respond everywhere you’ll hate it fast. If you’re hosting on a VPS and you care about hardening (you should) read How to host OpenClaw securely on a VPS. You don’t need to “code a Discord bot”. You create a Discord application, turn on a couple of intents, invite it to your server and give OpenClaw the bot token. ## Create the Discord bot and copy the token. Quick nuance: if you keep “require mention” on in busy channels you can often reduce how much message content the bot needs to see. ## Turn on Developer Mode and copy IDs. To build safe allowlists you’ll want IDs for your server (guild) and the channel(s) you want OpenClaw to listen to.

title: 🦞 Discord - OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Discord. OpenClaw connects to Discord as a bot over the official Discord gateway. DMs and guild channels are supported. Discord DMs default to pairing mode. Create a Discord application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it with OpenClaw. Use a private server if you can; create one first (**Create My Own > For me and my friends**) if needed. ### Create a Discord application and bot. In the Discord Developer Portal, click **New Application** and name it (for example "OpenClaw"). Open **Bot** in the sidebar and set the **Username** to your agent's name. On the **Bot** page, click **Reset Token** and copy the token. Keep the Server ID and User ID with your bot token; you need all three next. If your host is blocked or rate-limited by Discord's startup application lookup, set the application/client ID from the Developer Portal so startup can skip that REST call: `channels.discord.applicationId` for the default account, or `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.applicationId` per bot.

title: 🦞 Multi-Channel Bots with OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord & Slack - Learn OpenClaw - Resource Center
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Multi-Channel Bots with OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord & Slack. Imagine this: Your support team gets questions on Telegram. Your dev team lives in Discord. Your sales team uses Slack. Instead of running three separate chatbots (and three separate AI bills), you run **one OpenClaw agent** that responds intelligently across all three platforms simultaneously. OpenClaw’s `message` tool supports multiple channel plugins out of the box. In this ultimate guide, you’ll master everything from basic setup to advanced multi-agent architectures, security hardening, and production deployment strategies. By the end of this guide, you’ll have a production-ready multi-channel system that can:. * Receive and route messages intelligently across platforms. * Bind user identities across channels for unified conversations. Your team might live in Discord. Your agent doesn’t care whether the message came from Telegram or Discord—it just responds. If you’re hosting on a VPS (recommended for production), this means uptime, predictable networking, and isolation from your personal machine. * Bot tokens from each platform you want to support.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Scheduled tasks
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Cron is the Gateway's built-in scheduler. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output to a chat channel, a webhook, or nowhere. `openclaw cron create "2027-02-01T16:00:00Z" \ --name "Reminder" \ --session main \ --system-event "Reminder: check the cron docs draft" \ --wake now \ --delete-after-run`. `openclaw cron listopenclaw cron get <job-id>openclaw cron show <job-id>`. `openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>`. Once the runtime stops owning the job and a 5-minute grace window expires, maintenance checks persisted run logs and job state for the matching `cron:<jobId>:<startedAt>` run. To require both conditions, use croner's `+` day-of-week modifier (`0 9 15 * +1`), or schedule on one field and guard the other in your job's prompt or command. On `cron edit`, removes the per-job model override so the job follows normal cron model precedence (stored cron-session override, else agent/default model). If a job has `--model` but no explicit or configured fallback list, OpenClaw passes an empty fallback override instead of silently appending the agent primary as a hidden retry target.

title: 🦞 Cron Jobs & Sub-Agents - Introduction to OpenClaw | Stanza
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron Jobs & Sub-Agents. These features let you schedule recurring tasks and spawn child sessions that communicate with each other, enabling sophisticated automation workflows. `// Creating a cron job using the cron tool. // The job is persisted with key: cron:dependency-check`. The `cron.create` call registers a job with a unique `id`, a standard cron `schedule` expression, the `command` to execute, and any `context` the job needs. The job is stored under the key `cron:dependency-check` and will persist across sessions, meaning it continues to run even after you close and reopen OpenClaw. // Returns: [{ id: 'dependency-check', schedule: '0 * * * *', ... const job = await cron.get('dependency-check');. Cron jobs can run in two modes: in the **main session** (where the job's output is added to the next heartbeat turn) or in an **isolated session** (where the job gets its own fresh context window). `// A cron job that spawns a sub-agent for nightly analysis.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - 超智諮詢
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.

# OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Cron
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron. # `openclaw cron`. Run `openclaw cron --help` for the full command surface. `openclaw cron create` is an alias for `openclaw cron add`. Use `--webhook <url>` when the job should POST the finished payload instead of delivering to a chat target:. Use `--command` for deterministic shell-style jobs that run inside OpenClaw cron without starting an isolated agent/model run:. Command jobs capture stdout/stderr, record normal cron history, and route output through the same `announce`, `webhook`, or `none` delivery modes as isolated jobs. `openclaw cron list` and `openclaw cron show <job-id>` preview the resolved delivery route. Isolated `cron add` jobs default to `--announce` delivery. Isolated cron chat delivery is shared between the agent and the runner:. Use `cron add|create --webhook <url>` or `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>` to set webhook delivery. `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>` force-runs by default and returns as soon as the manual run is queued. `openclaw cron run <job-id>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> --run-id <run-id>`.
